title: Concurrency, Cancellation, Timeouts, Retries, and Flake Control
description: Two deployments race for one environment, an older run completes last, cancelled jobs skip cleanup, a hung process consumes the runner limit, and retries erase the first failure. Learners cumulatively produce bounded execution and concurrency policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Build concurrency keys from workflow, branch, pull request, service, or environment identity and choose cancellation from business semantics.
Prevent stale runs and out-of-order artifacts from overwriting newer deployments through revision, environment, and compare-before-mutate checks.
Set bounded job, step, command, network, and shutdown timeouts while terminating child processes and retaining partial evidence.
